behaviors.description: The keyword-frequency analyzer assigns the correct sentiment when the review contains only words from the configured positive and negative word lists.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: logic error in token counting/threshold comparison. Required setup: call the analyzer function with controlled word lists and reviews; no module handoff, OS, network, subprocess, or model-quality dependency is needed. Rejected Integration/System/Agentic because behavior text is limited to keyword-list scoring logic inside one analyzer.
behaviors.test_description: Construct table-driven unit cases with configured positive/negative lists and thresholds, run analyzer directly, and assert exact sentiment class for each input.

behaviors.description: The explanation analyzer produces reasoning that references specific phrases from the original review, not generic boilerplate.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: AI judgment-quality error (grounding/faithfulness of generated explanation). Required setup: run the real explanation generator and evaluate whether output cites review phrases; mocked/stubbed text generation would hide this failure. Rejected Workflow because this behavior targets one analyzer output, not multi-agentic-step orchestration.
behaviors.test_description: Send real reviews to the production explanation analyzer and assert output includes anchored phrase references from the input (with rubric-based checks across repeated runs).

behaviors.description: When a review contains mixed signals (both positive and negative keywords), the sentiment score reflects the configured tie-breaking rule.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: deterministic rule implementation error in tie-break logic. Required setup: direct analyzer invocation with mixed-keyword inputs and known config. Rejected higher levels because behavior text is specifically about configured tie-breaking inside keyword-frequency scoring, not external infra or AI output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Create unit tests where positive/negative counts tie or nearly tie under different threshold/tie-break configs and assert expected sentiment class.

behaviors.description: The explanation for a sarcastic review (e.g., "Great, another broken feature") correctly identifies the negative intent despite positive surface words.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: AI judgment-quality error on sarcasm/intent detection. Required setup: real model inference on sarcastic text; deterministic mocks or templates would not expose semantic misinterpretation. Rejected Workflow because this is a single explanation-generation judgment, not a chain of agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Run sarcasm-focused evaluation cases through the real explanation analyzer and verify explanations indicate negative intent and cite sarcasm cues, tracking pass rate over multiple runs.

behaviors.description: Both outputs (score and explanation) are returned together in a single response, with the explanation referencing the same sentiment direction as the score.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: module orchestration plus real-generation consistency error (score/explanation contradiction). Required setup: execute full response assembly with real explanation generation and verify directional alignment; stubbing explanation text can hide real contradictions even if wiring is correct. Rejected Workflow because there is only one agentic generation step plus deterministic scoring, not multiple agentic steps in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the end-to-end service with real explanation generation, assert response includes both fields in one payload, and check explanation sentiment direction matches computed score across a representative corpus.
